Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine and chloride, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria, suggesting impaired renal function with possible metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals protein, glucose, ketones, and abnormal appearance, supporting renal pathology (e.g., nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy), though HbA1c is low. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal and metabolic status.
